name: revenue-diagnostics
description: Figure out why revenue is declining. Extract the data, detect anomalies, decompose the problem, evaluate pricing, and produce an action plan. Stop guessing, start diagnosing.

config.instruction: Extract revenue data: monthly revenue trend, customer count trend, churn rate, average deal size, sales pipeline, top customers by revenue, revenue by product/segment. Identify the time period when decline started.

Detect anomalies in the revenue data. Look for: sudden drops, trend breaks, seasonal deviations, customer concentration risk, churn spikes, deal size compression, and pipeline velocity changes. Flag the top 3-5 anomalies by impact.

Decompose the revenue decline into root causes. Is it: fewer new customers? Higher churn? Smaller deals? Pricing pressure? Market shift? Competitive loss? Seasonal? One-time event? Rank causes by contribution to the decline.
Evaluate whether pricing is a factor. Compare current pricing to market, assess price elasticity, model what happens if you raise/lower prices by 10-20%. Recommend pricing adjustments if warranted.

Build a prioritized action plan to reverse the revenue decline. For each action: expected impact, effort required, timeline, owner. Separate into quick wins (this week), medium-term (this month), and strategic (this quarter).
